mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving immediate positive price adjustments in the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter as corporate revenues expand.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.01 for 5-day forward returns, or positive industrial growth coincides with negative equity returns in over 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal Rank IC drops below 0.03 across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the joint signal shows no out-of-sample predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes yield a Rank IC below 0.02 for 60-day forward returns, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns in over 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year industrial production contraction below zero percent indicates severe supply-side weakness and declining factory activity, triggering negative equity returns as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where INDPRO YoY is negative, or the threshold signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward tests.
